title: Realme C83 5G with 7,000mAh battery and Dimensity 6300 SoC launched in india, price starts at ₹13,499
summary: Realme C83 5G is launched in India featuring a 7000mAh battery, 6.7-inch LCD display, and MediaTek Dimensity 6300 processor. The phone starts at a price of ₹13,499.

title: Motorola Edge 70 Fusion with 7,000mAh battery, Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 SoC launched in India: Price, specs and more
summary: Motorola Edge 70 Fusion with 6.78-inch AMOLED display, Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, and a 7000mAh battery has been launched in India. The phone starts at a price of ₹ ₹26,999 and goes up to ₹32,999 for the top end variant.

title: WhatsApp could soon offer a paid subscription tier with exclusive stickers and ringtones: Report
summary: WhatsApp is reportedly developing a premium subscription plan called WhatsApp Plus. The new plan could offer features like customized app icon options, increased chat pinning, exclusive ringtones, and stickers.&nbsp;

title: Meta Ray-Bans may have exposed your most intimate moments: Here's what every owner must do immediately
summary: Meta is facing scrutiny for its privacy policies regarding user data collection through devices like Ray-Ban Smart Glasses. Cybersecurity experts highlight loopholes that compromise data safety, urging users to disable unnecessary sharing and be vigilant about their recordings.

title: Nothing Phone 4a with Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, Glyph Bar launched in India: Price, specs and more
summary: Nothing has launched the Phone (4a), a mid-range smartphone featuring a 50MP periscope telephoto lens and a 6.78-inch AMOLED display. The phone offers a Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, 12GB RAM, and a 5,400mAh battery, available in four colors via Flipkart.

title: Apple launches Mac Neo at ₹69,900: Top features, specs and how it compares to MacBook Air
summary: Apple's MacBook Neo is an entry-level laptop aimed at students, featuring a 13-inch Liquid Retina display, A18 Pro processor, and macOS Tahoe. It offers 16 hours of battery life, a 1080p FaceTime HD camera, and weighs 2.7 pounds, making it a competitive option against the MacBook Air and Pro.

title: MacBook price drop in 2026: Why buying the M3 and M4 models is a smart choice right now
summary: Maximize your 2026 savings with our guide to the MacBook price crash. Learn why the M3 and M4 are now the smartest buys for your budget and how to avoid the new model price trap.

title: Nothing Phone 4a Launch Highlights: Nothing Phone 4a, Phone 4a Pro launched in India
summary: Nothing Phone 4a Launch Highlights: Nothing has launched the Phone 4a, Phone 4a Pro in India alongside the new Headphone (a).

title: Realme Narzo Power 5G with Dimensity 7400 SoC, 10,001 mAh battery launched in India: Price, specs and more
summary: Realme launched the Narzo Power 5G in India featuring a 10,001 mAh battery, Dimensity 7400 processor, and priced from ₹27,999. First sale offers include discounts and a free 4-year battery warranty. The smartphone features a 6.8-inch AMOLED display and robust water resistance ratings.

title: Apple launches its lowest priced MacBook. Why it’s such a big deal.
summary: The MacBook Neo is less expensive than the iPhone 17.

title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei apologises for memo after ‘disorienting’ crisis
summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has apologised for how he handled a crisis after an internal memo strained the company’s relationship with the US government. In an interview with The Economist, he said events involving Donald Trump, the Department of War, and another AI provider unfolded within hours.

title: OpenAI launches GPT‑5.4 Thinking and Pro, its ‘most factual and efficient’ model yet
summary: OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.4 Thinking and GPT-5.4 Pro, the newest upgrades to its GPT-5 AI models. The company says the model is more factual and token-efficient, with faster responses, improved research abilities, stronger context retention, better benchmark scores, and lower error rates, alongsid

title: Karnataka bans social media for children under 16
summary: ​The ​southern Indian state of ​Karnataka, home to the tech hub of Bengaluru, ‌has ⁠banned ⁠the use of ​social media by those under ​the age of 16, state Chief Minister ​Siddaramaiah said ⁠on Friday, ‌becoming ​the ​first in India ⁠to do so.

title: SoftBank seeks up to $40 billion loan to finance OpenAI investment
summary: SoftBank is seeking up to $40 billion in loans, mainly to back its $30 billion investment in OpenAI. The Japanese firm has sold assets, increased debt, and made other AI-related acquisitions, raising concerns about liquidity, credit quality, and exposure amid an uncertain AI market and possible bubb

title: Anthropic to challenge Pentagon's ‘supply chain risk’ label
summary: CEO Dario Amodei said that Anthropic has received a letter from the US Department of War stating the AI company is considered a supply chain risk to America’s national security. Amodei said the firm believes the action is not legally sound and plans to challenge it in court.

title: US mulls new rules for AI chip exports, including requiring US investments by foreign firms
summary: United States officials are considering new rules to control exports of AI chips, possibly requiring foreign nations to invest in U.S. data centres or provide security guarantees. The proposal could affect sales by Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices, marking a shift from the approach under Joe Biden.

title: Amazon down for thousands of users in US: Downdetector
summary: Amazon experienced a website outage affecting thousands of users in the United States. Reports on Downdetector showed problems with logging in, browsing and checkout. Minor disruptions also appeared on Prime Video and Amazon Web Services, while the exact cause of the issue remained unclear.

title: Oracle layoffs planned as data centre costs surge: Bloomberg
summary: Oracle plans thousands of job cuts as it struggles with cash pressure from a huge AI data-centre expansion. The cloud company is investing heavily to support deals with OpenAI, xAI and Meta. Investors worry about rising debt, while hiring slows and some roles may shrink due to AI.

title: Anthropic deemed supply chain risk 'effective immediately': Pentagon
summary: The Pentagon has labelled Anthropic a supply-chain risk after its CEO Dario Amodei refused military use that could enable surveillance or autonomous weapons. Some contractors may stop using Claude. Critics call the action an overreach, while downloads surge and rivalry with OpenAI intensifies.

title: Banned RMG games thrive in India; green shoots in tech hiring
summary: Happy Friday! Accessing illegal offshore betting platforms continues to be easy for Indian users. This and more in today’s ETtech Morning Dispatch.
